name: Zapier MCP Server (Official)
what_it_does: Official Zapier MCP server enabling AI agents to trigger Zapier workflows (Zaps) and AI Actions — connecting AI agents to 6,000+ apps via Zapier's no-code automation platform.
use_cases: Triggering Zapier workflows from AI agents to automate cross-app actions
Sending data to any of 6,000+ apps via Zapier AI Actions
Creating CRM contacts, calendar events, or tickets from agent decisions
Automating notifications across Slack, email, and other apps from agents
Running pre-built multi-step workflows on behalf of AI agents
not_for: Complex custom logic (Zapier is limited to configured Zap actions)
High-frequency automation (Zapier has task limits by plan)
Sensitive data workflows requiring on-premises processing
best_when: An agent needs to trigger actions in external apps without native API integrations — Zapier's 6,000+ app library provides unmatched breadth.
avoid_when: You need direct API control over the target app — use the app's native MCP or API for better reliability and less latency overhead.

auth.notes: Zapier NLA (Natural Language Actions) uses API key. OAuth for full Zapier account access. Actions must be pre-configured in Zapier dashboard.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Free: 100 tasks/month, 5 Zaps, limited to 2-step Zaps
pricing.notes: Task-based pricing. AI Actions included in applicable plans. MCP server is open source.

performance.rate_limits: Task limits by plan; NLA API rate limits not prominently documented

known_agent_gotchas: Actions must be pre-configured in Zapier dashboard — agents cannot create new Zaps on the fly
High latency (seconds) due to multi-step Zap execution — agents must handle async
Non-idempotent by design — duplicate triggers create duplicate Zap runs
Task limits are per-month — agents can exhaust monthly budget quickly
Zapier adds a layer of unreliability — if any step in a Zap fails, action fails silently
NLA Actions are a subset of Zapier capabilities — not all Zap actions are NLA-accessible
agent_readiness.security_notes: HTTPS enforced. API key auth with no scopes. Data flows through Zapier's infrastructure — sensitive data should not flow through Zapier without reviewing their data policies. SOC 2 Type II.

security_score_components.security_notes: HTTPS enforced. Data passes through Zapier servers — review data handling for sensitive use cases. No scope granularity. SOC 2 Type II.
